name: Funded Trading Plus
description_short: UK-based prop firm founded in 2013 offering instant funding and evaluation programs with up to 100% profit splits, 5 trading platforms, and $19.5M+ paid out to 60,000+ traders worldwide.
description_long:
## What Funded Trading Plus Is

Funded Trading Plus is a UK-based proprietary trading firm that gives retail traders access to simulated capital in exchange for a share of the profits. Founded in 2013 and operated by FTP London Ltd. out of Manchester, United Kingdom, it is one of the longer-running names in the prop firm space — most of its competitors were spun up during the 2021-2023 prop firm boom, while Funded Trading Plus has been iterating on its model for over a decade.

The company reports over 60,000 funded traders and has paid out more than $19.5 million to date. In a category where roughly half the firms that launched in 2022 have already collapsed, gone insolvent, or been shut down by regulators, that longevity matters. Funded Trading Plus positions itself primarily to forex, indices, commodities, and crypto CFD traders — not futures or equities traders.

## Core Features and What Makes It Different

Funded Trading Plus runs three distinct funding paths, which is more flexibility than most prop firms offer. You can pick a **2-Step Classic** evaluation, a **1-Step Express** evaluation, or skip evaluation entirely with **Instant Funding**. That third option is what separates Funded Trading Plus from firms like FTMO or The Funded Trader — most prop firms require you to pass at least one profit target before touching any capital.

The platform selection is also unusually broad. Where most prop firms lock you into MetaTrader 4 and 5, Funded Trading Plus supports five trading platforms including cTrader and DXTrade. If you have a strong preference for a specific platform (cTrader traders in particular know the pain of being forced back onto MT4/5), this matters.

Other features worth naming: profit splits scale from 80% up to **100%** through the growth program, scaling accounts can climb to **$2.5 million** in funded capital, payouts run every 7 to 10 calendar days depending on the plan, and the one-time evaluation fee is refunded with your first payout — a detail that softens the sticker shock. Instant Funding accounts also carry no swap fees.

## Pricing Breakdown

Funded Trading Plus pricing starts at **$119** for entry-level evaluation accounts. Here is the actual breakdown:

**2-Step Classic** starts at $119. This is the traditional two-phase evaluation with a 3% daily drawdown and 6% total drawdown. Accounts scale up to $200,000. Profit split is 80%, scalable to 100%. Payouts arrive every 10 calendar days. The fee is refunded with your first payout.

**1-Step Express** also starts at $119. Single-phase evaluation, 10% profit target, accounts up to $200,000. Payouts start from Day 1 and arrive every 7 days. Same 80%-to-100% profit split. Fee refunded with first payout.

**Instant Funding** starts at $299. No evaluation required — you trade from Day 1. Accounts range from $5,000 to $100,000 (smaller ceiling than the evaluation routes). Up to 80% profit split. Weekly payouts. No swap fees.

At $119 entry-level, Funded Trading Plus is more expensive than FTMO's cheapest $10K challenge (around $89) and materially more expensive than Apex Trader Funding's frequent 80%-off promotions. The $299 Instant Funding entry point is where things get expensive — you are paying upfront to skip the evaluation risk. Prices vary by account size; check their website for current pricing on the specific tier you want.

## Honest Pros

**Longevity.** Founded in 2013, Funded Trading Plus survived the 2023-2024 prop firm shakeout that killed dozens of competitors. That is not marketing — that is a track record.

**Real Instant Funding option.** You can genuinely skip evaluation. This is rare and useful for experienced traders who do not want to gamble a fee on hitting an arbitrary profit target under time pressure.

**100% profit split ceiling.** Most firms cap at 80% or 90%. Funded Trading Plus scales all the way to 100% via its growth program.

**Platform flexibility.** Five platforms including cTrader and DXTrade is a genuine differentiator versus MT4/MT5-only competitors.

**Scaling to $2.5M.** For traders who actually scale into consistent profitability, the ceiling is high enough to build a real career on.

**Fee refund on first payout.** Turns the evaluation fee into a deposit rather than a sunk cost, assuming you make it to a payout.

## Honest Cons

**Entry prices are on the higher end.** $119 for the smallest evaluation and $299 for Instant Funding puts Funded Trading Plus above discount-heavy competitors like Apex Trader Funding, which regularly runs 80%-90% off promotions.

**No native mobile app.** You are relying on the underlying broker platform apps (MT4, MT5, cTrader, DXTrade mobile) rather than a Funded Trading Plus-branded mobile experience. Not a dealbreaker, but worth naming.

**Everything is a CFD.** No direct access to stocks, no futures. If you are a futures trader, Topstep or Apex Trader Funding are the correct comparisons, not Funded Trading Plus.

**Post-evaluation rule enforcement.** Trustpilot and Reddit reviews consistently mention that funded-phase rules are enforced strictly, and some traders report account terminations for edge-case rule interpretations. Read the rulebook line by line before you scale up.

**CFDs mean regional access issues.** Depending on your country, CFDs may be restricted or unavailable — US retail traders in particular have historically had complicated access to CFD-based prop firms.

## Who Should Use Funded Trading Plus

Funded Trading Plus fits **forex, indices, commodities, and crypto CFD traders** — particularly those based in the UK, EU, and other regions where CFD trading is fully accessible. It also fits **experienced traders who want to skip evaluation** and can justify the $299 Instant Funding fee. If you want **platform choice beyond MT4/MT5**, especially cTrader or DXTrade, this is one of the few prop firms that will accommodate you. And if you are **building toward large funded capital**, the $2.5M scaling ceiling and 100% profit split are competitive.

Who should NOT use it: **futures traders** (go to Topstep or Apex Trader Funding instead), **US-based traders concerned about CFD availability**, **very cost-sensitive traders** (Apex's promo pricing will be cheaper), and **traders who need a polished native mobile app**.

## How It Compares

**Funded Trading Plus vs. FTMO:** FTMO is the incumbent — larger, better known, similar evaluation model. FTMO does not offer true instant funding. Funded Trading Plus does. FTMO's rules are considered more forgiving; Funded Trading Plus's rules are enforced strictly. Both are strong choices; the tiebreaker is usually whether you want instant funding (FTP) or maximum brand trust (FTMO).

**Funded Trading Plus vs. Apex Trader Funding:** Different products despite the shared category. Apex is futures-focused; Funded Trading Plus is CFD-focused. Apex runs aggressive discounts. Funded Trading Plus does not. If you trade futures, Apex. If you trade forex/indices/crypto CFDs and value platform flexibility, Funded Trading Plus.

## The Bottom Line

Funded Trading Plus is one of the more credible prop firms in the CFD space — over a decade of operation, $19.5M+ in payouts, and a genuinely differentiated Instant Funding path. It is not the cheapest option, and if you want futures or a native mobile app, look elsewhere. But for forex, indices, commodities, and crypto CFD traders who want platform flexibility, a real path to 100% profit splits, and the option to skip evaluation entirely, Funded Trading Plus is a serious contender worth putting on a shortlist alongside FTMO. Rated 4.4/5.
